title: agrimonia eupatoria subsp. asiatica
intro: Agrimonia eupatoria subsp. asiatica, also known as Asiatic agrimony or Chinese agrimony, is a subspecies of the Agrimonia eupatoria plant, a flowering plant native to East Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The medicinal properties of Agrimonia eupatoria subsp. asiatica have been found to provide various health benefits, including reducing bleeding, inflammation, and pain, as well as supporting the immune system and promoting wound healing. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or capsules, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
